skills.name: ftm
skills.description: Universal entry point for all ftm skills. Routes freeform text to the right ftm skill. ftm-mind is the default cognitive entry point for all unclassified input.

skip blackboard update, proceed with routing

fallbacks.condition: skill tool unavailable for target skill
fallbacks.action: report routing failure to user with the target skill name

skills.name: ftm-audit
skills.description: Dual-purpose wiring audit that verifies all code is actually connected to the running application. Combines static analysis (knip) with adversarial LLM audit and auto-fixes anything it finds. Use when user says "audit", "wiring check", "verify wiring", "dead code", "check imports", "unused code", "find dead code", or "audit wiring". Also auto-invoked by ftm-executor after each task.

risk.scope: modifies source files to fix wiring issues (auto-fix layer); also adds/removes imports and route registrations; reads codebase broadly
risk.rollback: git checkout on auto-fixed files; all changes are tracked in the changelog report before being applied

approval_gates.action: report proposed change before applying (show "Proposed: ..." format)
surface to user with suggested action, do not auto-fix
stop and report remaining issues to user

fallbacks.condition: knip not installed and npx unavailable
fallbacks.action: skip Layer 1, run Layer 2 adversarial audit only
skip knip entirely, run adversarial audit only
skip Phase 3 runtime wiring check, log reason and continue
skip Phase 3 runtime wiring check, log reason and continue
run pure Layer 1 + Layer 2 analysis without contract checking

fallbacks.condition: project has no identifiable entry point
fallbacks.action: skip knip, run adversarial audit only

skills.name: ftm-brainstorm
skills.description: Research-powered Socratic brainstorming that dispatches parallel agents to search the web and GitHub for real-world patterns, then synthesizes findings into actionable suggestions with citations. Use this skill whenever the user wants to brainstorm, explore ideas, think through a feature, plan a project, or flesh out a concept before building. Also triggers when the user pastes a large block of text (notes, prior brainstorm, meeting transcript, spec draft, stream-of-consciousness dump) and wants to turn it into something buildable — phrases like "help me build this", "turn this into a plan", "here's what I've been thinking", or just a big paste followed by "what do you think?" or "go". Triggers on "brainstorm", "help me think through", "I have an idea for", "how should I approach", "let's explore", "what if we built", "I'm thinking about", "help me figure out", or any conversation where the user has a concept they want to develop before writing code. Even vague ideas like "I want to build something that..." or "what's the best way to..." should trigger this skill.

risk.scope: writes plan documents to ~/.claude/plans/; writes blackboard context and experience files; does not modify project source code
risk.rollback: delete generated plan file; blackboard writes can be reverted by editing JSON files

approval_gates.action: present "Here's what I think we've landed on" summary and wait for explicit user approval before generating plan
present plan incrementally (vision → tasks → agents/waves) and get approval at each step

approval_gates.trigger: research returns thin results on all agents
approval_gates.action: note research gaps, present fewer suggestions, do not fabricate citations

fallbacks.action: dispatch 3 direct parallel research agents (web/github/competitive) using built-in prompts from references/agent-prompts.md

fallbacks.condition: no git repo detected in Phase 0
fallbacks.action: skip repo scan, ask about tech stack during intake
proceed without experience-informed shortcuts, rely on direct analysis
use session default model for all agents

capabilities.name: WebSearch
capabilities.description: web research agents use for blog posts and case studies

plan_generated.description: absolute path to generated plan file

skills.name: ftm-browse
skills.description: Headless browser daemon for visual verification and web interaction. Gives agents the ability to navigate, screenshot, click, fill forms, and inspect ARIA trees via CLI commands. Use when user says "browse", "screenshot", "visual", "look at the app", "open browser", "check the page", "navigate to", "take a screenshot", "visual verification".

risk.scope: navigates browser, takes screenshots saved to ~/.ftm-browse/screenshots/; does not modify project source files; form fills and clicks can have side effects on the target application
risk.rollback: no project file mutations; browser interactions on local dev servers are typically reversible by reloading

approval_gates.action: STOP immediately, present options (retry/skip/abort/manual), wait for user choice

approval_gates.action: STOP, take screenshot, present situation to user, wait for explicit choice

approval_gates.trigger: supervised mode enabled AND state mismatch detected
approval_gates.action: halt and report before proceeding

fallbacks.condition: dev server not running when navigating to localhost

re-run snapshot -i before retrying click/fill

skills.name: ftm-codex-gate
skills.description: Codex CLI integration gate for adversarial code validation. Invokes codex exec --yolo --ephemeral with gpt-5.4 to form test scenarios, review code quality, fix failures, and enforce STYLE.md patterns. Use when ftm-executor needs Codex validation at wave boundaries or task completion, or when user says "codex gate", "run codex", "validate with codex". Not for direct user interaction — primarily auto-invoked by ftm-executor.

skills.name: ftm-council
skills.description: Multi-AI deliberation council that sends problems to Claude, Codex, and Gemini as equal peers, then loops through rounds of debate until 2-of-3 agree on a decision. Use when the user wants a second (and third) opinion, says "council this", "get other opinions", "what would other AIs think", "debate this", "multi-model", "ftm-council", or wants to cross-check a decision, architecture choice, debugging approach, or any problem where diverse AI perspectives would reduce blind spots. Especially valuable for debugging hard problems, architecture decisions, code review, and any situation where confirmation bias from a single model is a risk. Even if the user just says "I'm not sure about this approach" or "sanity check this", consider invoking the council.

skills.name: ftm-debug
skills.description: Deep multi-vector debugging war room that launches parallel agent teams to instrument, research, reproduce, hypothesize, solve, and verify tricky bugs. Use when a bug is stubborn, multi-turn debugging hasn't worked, the user says "debug this deeply", "war room this", "I can't figure out why", "this is driving me crazy", "launch the debug team", or any situation where standard debugging is insufficient. Also triggers on "/ftm-debug". Covers any codebase — frontend, backend, CLI tools, native apps, build systems, anything. Do NOT use for simple one-step fixes — this is the heavy artillery for problems that resist normal debugging.

skills.name: ftm-executor
skills.description: Autonomous plan execution engine. Takes any plan document and executes it end-to-end with a dynamically assembled agent team — analyzing tasks, creating purpose-built agents, dispatching them in parallel worktrees, and running each through a commit-review-fix loop until complete. Use this skill whenever the user wants to execute a plan, run a plan doc, launch an agent team on tasks, or says things like "execute this plan", "run this", "launch agents on this doc", "take this plan and go", or points to a plan file and wants it implemented autonomously. Even if they just paste a plan path and say "go" — this is the skill.

skills.name: ftm-git
skills.description: Secret scanning and credential safety gate for git operations. Prevents API keys, tokens, passwords, and other secrets from ever being committed or pushed to remote repositories. Scans staged files, working tree, and git history for hardcoded credentials using regex pattern matching, then auto-remediates by extracting secrets to gitignored .env files and replacing hardcoded values with env var references. Use when user says "scan for secrets", "check for keys", "audit credentials", "ftm-git", "secret scan", "remove api keys", "check before push", or any time git commit/push operations are about to happen. Also auto-invoked by ftm-executor and ftm-mind before any commit or push operation. Even if the user just says "commit this" or "push to remote", this skill MUST run first. Do NOT use for general git workflow operations like branching or merging — that's git-workflow territory. This skill is specifically the security gate.

requirements.description: remediation protocol, env var patterns, report formats

skills.name: ftm-mind
skills.description: Unified OODA cognitive loop for the ftm system. Use for freeform `/ftm` or `/ftm-mind` requests, vague asks, mixed-tool workflows, Jira/ticket-driven work, or any request that should be understood before routing. Also handles explicit ftm skill invocations by honoring the requested skill while still doing a fast orientation pass for context, prerequisites, and approval gates. Triggers on open-ended requests like "help me think through this", bug reports, plan execution asks, Jira URLs, "make this better", mixed MCP asks like "check my calendar and draft a Slack message", and direct skill invocations like "/ftm-debug ..." or "/ftm-brainstorm ...". skills.name: ftm-researcher
skills.description: Deep parallel research engine with 7 domain-specialized finder agents, adversarial review via ftm-council, adaptive wave-based search, structured reconciliation with disagreement maps, credibility scoring, and conversational iteration. Use when the user wants thorough research on any topic — "research X", "find out about Y", "what's the state of the art on Z", "compare approaches to W", "deep dive into X", "look into Y". Also invoked by ftm-brainstorm for its research sprints. Triggers on "research", "investigate", "deep dive", "state of the art", "compare", "find examples of", "what's out there for", "how do others handle", "find me evidence", "look into". For idea exploration and brainstorming, use ftm-brainstorm instead (which calls ftm-researcher internally for research).

requirements.name: ~/.claude/ftm-config.yml
requirements.description: planning and review model profiles, per_skill_overrides.ftm-researcher agent cap

requirements.name: ftm-researcher/references/agent-prompts.md
requirements.description: 7 finder agent prompts and orchestrator decomposition protocol

risk.scope: reads web sources and local codebase via agents; writes blackboard experience entry; writes structured JSON artifact; does not modify project source files
risk.rollback: no project mutations; blackboard write can be reverted by editing JSON files

approval_gates.action: finalize, write blackboard, emit events
council runs automatically on top claims (no user gate needed for this step)

fallbacks.action: use 2 fallback challenger agents (Devil's Advocate + Edge Case Hunter) instead
queue excess agents and dispatch after current wave completes
broaden query and retry; if still empty, report "No prior art found — this may be novel"
proceed without experience-informed shortcuts

capabilities.name: WebSearch
capabilities.description: finder agents for web, GitHub, and competitive research

capabilities.name: WebFetch
capabilities.description: fetching specific URLs found during research

skills.name: ftm-resume
skills.description: Resume a previously paused ftm skill session from saved state. Use when user says "resume", "continue where we left off", "ftm resume", "pick up", or starts a new conversation wanting to continue previous ftm work.

requirements.name: ~/.claude/ftm-state/STATE.md
requirements.description: saved session state file from ftm-pause

requirements.name: ../ftm-pause/references/protocols/VALIDATION.md
requirements.description: validation protocol for state file integrity

requirements.name: git
requirements.description: checking git state drift since session was paused

risk.scope: archives STATE.md by moving it to ~/.claude/ftm-state/archive/; invokes the target ftm skill with restored context; does not modify project source files
risk.rollback: copy archived STATE.md back from archive if restoration was incorrect

approval_gates.trigger: validation finds warnings (git drift, stale state, missing artifacts)
approval_gates.action: present consolidated validation summary and require user acknowledgment before proceeding
stop and report failure; do not invoke target skill

approval_gates.trigger: user provides new context along with "yes"
approval_gates.action: capture as post-pause update and inject into skill invocation

fallbacks.action: report "No saved ftm session found" with instructions for saving sessions
report validation failure with specific missing fields
ask user which to resume, list each with skill type and timestamp
flag as potentially stale with warning, require user acknowledgment

session_resumed.description: the ftm skill that was re-invoked
phase the session is resuming at
how long ago the session was paused

skills.name: ftm-retro
skills.description: Post-execution self-assessment skill. Automatically triggered after ftm-executor completes a plan. Scores execution across 5 dimensions, identifies what went well and what was slow, writes structured report with improvement suggestions. Use when user says "retro", "retrospective", "how did that go", "execution review", "self-assessment", "ftm retro".

requirements.name: PROGRESS.md
requirements.description: executor progress log for auto-triggered mode

requirements.name: ~/.claude/ftm-retros/
requirements.description: prior retro files for pattern analysis

requirements.name: references/protocols/SCORING-RUBRICS.md
requirements.description: scoring scale breakpoints and evidence requirements

requirements.name: ~/.claude/ftm-state/blackboard/patterns.json
requirements.description: pattern registry for promotion and decay

risk.scope: writes retro report to ~/.claude/ftm-retros/; writes experience files to blackboard; promotes patterns to patterns.json; does not modify project source files
risk.rollback: delete retro report file; remove experience entry from blackboard

approval_gates.action: auto-promote to patterns.json without user gate (learning system behavior)

fallbacks.condition: PROGRESS.md not found and manual mode
fallbacks.action: check ~/.claude/ftm-retros/ for most recent .md file; ask user which execution to review if multiple found

fallbacks.condition: execution context not provided by ftm-executor
fallbacks.action: reconstruct from PROGRESS.md or ask user for context
apply built-in scoring heuristics from skill body
cold-start mode — record every task, set all confidence to low

pattern_discovered.description: number of experiences that triggered promotion

task_completed.description: absolute path to saved retro report

skills.name: ftm-routine
skills.description: Execute named, recurring multi-step workflows from YAML definitions. Use when user says "routine", "run routine", "ftm-routine", "morning triage", or names a known routine.

risk.scope: executes MCP tool calls, skill invocations, and bash commands as defined by the routine; external-facing mutations depend entirely on routine definition; approval gates in the routine control which steps require user confirmation
risk.rollback: depends on individual routine steps; steps with approval: approve gate let user review before execution; MCP writes (Jira, Slack, email) may not be reversible

approval_gates.action: show plan for that step and wait for "go" before executing
execute step, show results, wait for "continue" or "stop"
execute automatically and show results

fallbacks.action: show "Routine not found" with list of available routines from ~/.ftm/routines/

fallbacks.condition: MCP tool referenced in routine not available
fallbacks.action: report unavailable tool, ask user whether to skip that step or abort

fallbacks.condition: skill referenced in routine not available
fallbacks.action: report unavailable skill, ask user whether to skip or abort

fallbacks.condition: bash command in step fails with non-zero exit
fallbacks.action: report failure output, ask user whether to continue or abort

skills.name: ftm-upgrade
skills.description: Self-upgrade mechanism for ftm skills ecosystem. Checks GitHub Releases for new versions, caches results for 60min, shows changelog, and copies updated files. Use when user says "upgrade", "update skills", "new version", "check for updates", "ftm upgrade".

requirements.name: gh
requirements.description: GitHub CLI for querying releases from kkudumu/ftm-brain

requirements.name: ~/.claude/skills/ftm-upgrade/scripts/upgrade.sh
requirements.description: download and install latest release script

risk.scope: downloads and overwrites skill files in ~/.claude/skills/ on upgrade; changes affect all ftm skill behavior going forward; irreversible without restoring previous version from backup or git
risk.rollback: restore from ~/.claude/skills/ backup if one was made before upgrade; or reinstall specific version by downloading an older release tarball

approval_gates.action: show current and latest version with changelog URL, wait for explicit "yes" confirmation before running upgrade.sh

approval_gates.trigger: version check during preamble (passive notice pattern)
approval_gates.action: show one-line notice only, do NOT ask for confirmation or interrupt workflow

fallbacks.action: report "GitHub CLI not installed" with brew install gh instructions
report "Cannot reach GitHub. Check internet connection."
report repo not found, suggest verifying access
report "No releases found yet. Check back later."
report failure output, suggest running script manually

capabilities.name: gh
capabilities.description: GitHub CLI for release queries and download
